rules.name: integration_patterns
rules.overview: The 4 choke-point pattern is the canonical integration model. This file gives the exact metric set + required fields + sample code per choke point, plus three reference integrations (Python library, REST, Node).

choke_point.name: 1_input
choke_point.purpose: Catch malicious or out-of-policy inputs before any expensive work.
choke_point.required_fields: input_text, plus system_prompt for Prompt Safety / Topic Relevance, plus params for Pattern metrics
choke_point.on_block: Refuse with the metric's fallback_message. Do NOT call the retriever or LLM.
choke_point.on_flag: Allow through; audit log records the borderline state for human review.
choke_point.example_code: bundle = ev.evaluate( input_text=user_query, categories=["safety", "pattern"], params={"keywords": partner_block_list}, ) if bundle.failed(): return bundle.failed()[0].fallback_message

choke_point.name: 2_retrieval
choke_point.purpose: Catch retrieval failures cheaply before paying for an LLM call on bad context.
choke_point.required_fields: input_text, context (must be list[str], one entry per retrieved doc)
choke_point.on_block: Skip the LLM. Return a "no relevant info" message.
choke_point.on_flag: Allow LLM call but log low-confidence retrieval for analysis.
choke_point.example_code: docs = vector_store.retrieve(user_query, k=5) # list[str] bundle = ev.evaluate( input_text=user_query, context=docs, categories=["rag_retrieval"], ) if bundle.failed(): return "I don't have enough relevant information to answer that."
choke_point.gotcha: The context field MUST be list[str] here, not a single string — RULE 6. If you pass a single string, the SDK treats it as one document and HitRate is trivially 0 or 1.

choke_point.name: 3_generation
choke_point.required_fields: input_text + generated_text + context (single string for the chunks the LLM saw)
choke_point.on_block: Regenerate once with a stricter prompt; if it blocks again, fall back to refusal.
choke_point.example_code: best_context = "\n\n".join(docs[:3]) answer = llm.generate(user_query, context=best_context) bundle = ev.evaluate( input_text=user_query, generated_text=answer, context=best_context, categories=["rag_generation"], )

choke_point.name: 4_output
choke_point.purpose: Final safety + quality gate on what the user actually sees.

recommended_metrics.metric: PII Detection (on generated_text — catches LLM leaks)
Conciseness, Text Grade Level, Text Reading Ease (optional quality)

choke_point.on_block: Scrub the offending span or regenerate.
choke_point.example_code: bundle = ev.evaluate( generated_text=answer, metrics=["PII Detection", "HAP (Hate, Abuse, Profanity)", "Conciseness (LLM Judge)"], ) if bundle.failed(): return bundle.failed()[0].fallback_message

integration_reference.name: python_library
integration_reference.when: Agent is a Python service and can pip-install into the same process.
integration_reference.one_class_summary:
from real_time_guardrails import GuardrailsEvaluator, AuditLogger from examples.full_pipeline import GuardrailedAgent
ev = GuardrailsEvaluator() audit = AuditLogger(path="audit.jsonl") agent = GuardrailedAgent(ev, audit=audit, retrieve_callback=my_retriever, model_callback=my_llm) result = agent.process_request(user_query, request_id="req-42") return result.final_response

integration_reference.name: rest_api
integration_reference.when: Agent is in another language (Node, Go, Java) or another service.
integration_reference.deploy: Run `real-time-guardrails serve --port 8090` next to the agent. Co-locate in the same VPC for latency.
integration_reference.request_shape: POST /api/evaluate { "input_text": "...", "generated_text": "...", // optional "context": "..." or [...], // string for RAG-gen, list for RAG-retr "system_prompt": "...", // optional "tool_calls": [{...}], // optional, OpenAI format "available_tools": [{...}], // optional "params": {...}, // optional, for Pattern metrics "metrics": ["..."], // OR "categories": ["..."], // OR omit both for auto-select "thresholds": {"metric": 0.5}, // optional block override "flag_thresholds": {"metric": 0.3}, // optional flag override "fallback_messages": {"metric": "..."}, // optional override "interaction_id": "req-42" }

integration_reference.name: mcp_tool
integration_reference.when: Agent is built on MCP and consumes guardrails as a tool.
integration_reference.deploy: Add the server to your MCP client's configuration. MCP (Model Context Protocol) clients are host applications — chat tools, IDE extensions, agent frameworks — that discover and invoke tools exposed by MCP servers. Configuration format varies; consult your client's documentation for the exact file path and schema. The block to add:

parallelization_pattern.when: Latency optimization for Python library users.
parallelization_pattern.pattern: Stages 1 (input safety) and 2 (retrieval) are independent — fire retrieval while the input check is in-flight, await both before the LLM call.
parallelization_pattern.example_code: import asyncio input_task = asyncio.create_task(asyncio.to_thread( ev.evaluate, input_text=query, categories=["safety"])) docs = await my_async_retriever(query) input_bundle = await input_task if input_bundle.failed(): return input_bundle.failed()[0].fallback_message

tool_call_payload_format.rule: OpenAI function-calling format ONLY — RULE 5. Flat dicts are rejected by ToolSpec validation.
tool_call_payload_format.incorrect_format: // WRONG — flat shape, no "function" wrapper: tool_calls = [{"name": "get_weather", "arguments": {"city": "Tokyo"}}]
